flashcards.definition: C(x) = fixed cost + variable cost terms; a polynomial in units produced x.
The additional cost to produce one more unit; approximated by the derivative of the cost function.
s(t) = s₀ + v₀t + ½at²; a degree-2 polynomial when acceleration is constant.
Finding the point(s) where revenue equals cost: R(x) = C(x).
C̄(x) = C(x)/x; the cost per unit, which decreases then increases (economies of scale).
